terms.definition: A complete binary tree that satisfies the heap invariant (min-heap: parent ≤ children; max-heap: parent ≥ children); usually stored as an array.
Heap variants where the root is the minimum or maximum element, respectively.
The ordering rule between parent and children; siblings remain unordered with respect to each other.
Operations that restore the heap invariant after an insert (sift up) or after replacing the root (sift down).
Convert an arbitrary array into a heap in O(n) using bottom-up sift-down.
An ADT that dequeues elements by priority instead of arrival order; commonly implemented with a heap.
The k largest (or smallest) elements from a (potentially streaming) collection — a flagship heap use case.
A decoding strategy that keeps the top-k partial hypotheses by score at each step; uses a heap to track them.
Merging k pre-sorted streams into one sorted stream using a heap of size k.
